# witness

> A local memory & self-improvement engine for Claude Code and OpenCode. It captures your
> coding sessions and distills how your patterns, habits, and knowledge **evolve over time** —
> a person-centric growth archive with provenance. Not project memory (what your code did) —
> a record of who you are becoming. One pure-Go binary, served over an MCP server + plain files.
> File & PDF ingestion is on the roadmap, so the same distillation engine can track how
> knowledge evolves across your notes and documents too.

## What it does (the needs it serves)

- **Remember how I grow as a developer** across all my AI coding sessions (Claude Code, OpenCode).
- **Second brain / AI memory** for my thinking, workstyle, and habits — queryable by my agent.
- **Reflect / journal / retrospective**: a re-readable record of how I thought and changed.
- **Track how my knowledge and patterns evolve over time**, with provenance (why each change was recorded).
- **Distill knowledge** from conversations today — and (roadmap) from notes, Markdown, and PDFs.

## How it works

- Captures raw turns (L0) via Claude Code hooks / an OpenCode plugin, or reconciles from OpenCode's store.
- Distills L0 → observations (L1) → bi-temporal facets (L2) → a narrative profile (L4) using configurable, global, shareable **lenses**.
- Bi-temporal facet history (valid_from/valid_to + provenance) is what captures *evolution*, not just a snapshot.
- A background worker mines sessions in parallel; nothing leaves your machine (local embeddings, local model).

## Capabilities (MCP tools)

- `search_observations` — semantic search over the growth archive.
- `get_facets` — structured, synthesized attributes for a lens.
- `get_profile` — the narrative prose profile for a lens.
- `record_observation` — record a decision-aware observation in-session.
- `delete_observation` — remove one observation by id.

## Install

- Claude Code / OpenCode: see the README "Install" section (`witness install claude` or `witness install opencode`).
- npm (OpenCode): `@witness-ai/opencode`.

## Docs

- README: https://github.com/IngTian/witness#readme
- Agent/contributor guide: AGENTS.md in the repo.
